How it all works, from day one.

A walk through the world you have just joined: the industry, where Sagepilot sits inside it, the channels we speak through, how we beat the competition, what we charge, the brands who trust us, and the people behind it.

Part 1

How the industry works

Imagine someone buys a toy from an online shop. From the moment they tap buy to the moment the box reaches their door, and sometimes back again, a team of little helper companies each does one small job. Every integration is a window. The more windows Sagepilot sees through, the more it knows.

Shop→Pay→Pack & ship→Deliver→Return→Keep the customer

Tap each stage. For every tool, the real question is not "what is this company," it is "what does Sagepilot get to see because of it."

Commerce, the shop itself

Shopify is the shop: the website where products sit, the price tags, the cash register, the list of who bought what. Unicommerce and EasyEcom are the back-room manager that keeps one honest count of stock across every place a brand sells and decides which warehouse ships each order.

ShopifyUnicommerceEasyEcom
What Sagepilot learns

Every product, every price, what is in stock, who the customer is, and their full order history. The foundation. Without it the AI is blind.

Identity and Payments, taking the money

Razorpay is the card machine: it collects the money and sends refunds. The checkout fixers all share one idea, the fast self-checkout lane. They pre-fill the address so buying is one tap, and they fight India's biggest headache, COD orders that never get accepted at the door (RTO).

RazorpayRazorpay Magic CheckoutGoKwikShopfloBreezeFastrrNitrocommerce
What Sagepilot learns

Was the order paid or is it COD, did a payment fail, is a refund pending, and the gold one, abandoned carts the AI can go win back.

Shipping, getting the box to the door

Two types only, and this is the most useful thing to learn. Couriers physically carry the box. Aggregators are a courier picker: one dashboard connected to many couriers that picks the cheapest or fastest, prints the label, tracks the parcel, and chases failed deliveries. Most aggregators do the same job, so learn the category, not fake differences.

Couriers
DelhiveryBlue DartShadowfaxEkart
Aggregators
ShiprocketShipwayNimbusPostiThinkClickPostProshipGoSwiftFShipRapidshypShipEaseShipCluesOpenLeafShipStation
What Sagepilot learns

Where the order is right now, the tracking link, the expected date, and whether a delivery failed. This is what answers “where is my order,” the most common question a brand gets.

Returns, sending it back or swapping

The returns desk. A self-serve page where a customer says “wrong size” or “damaged,” gets a return label, and picks refund, exchange, or store credit. A big part of their job is gently turning a refund into an exchange, so the brand keeps the money.

Return PrimeEcoReturnsQuickReturns
What Sagepilot learns

Did the customer raise a return, why, what stage it is at, and the refund or store-credit status. So the AI can handle “I want a bigger size” end to end.

Loyalty and Reviews, making them come back

Judge.me and Trustoo collect and show star ratings and reviews, the social proof that builds trust. Nector and Getflits run points, coins, VIP tiers, and referrals. ReferRush is referral programs specifically.

Judge.meTrustooNectorGetflitsReferRush
What Sagepilot learns

The customer's points balance and VIP tier, whether they left a review, and their referral activity. So the AI can say “you have 150 coins, want to use them,” at the perfect moment.

Webhooks and Ops, voice, video and the universal adapter

Exotel is the phone switchboard that powers real voice calls. Petpooja runs the counter and online orders for food businesses. Popin is live video shopping. Custom Webhook is the universal adapter that lets a brand feed in events from almost any tool. (Flexype and Ecom360 are really checkout tools that happen to live here.)

ExotelPetpoojaPopinCustom WebhookFlexypeEcom360
What Sagepilot learns

Phone conversations, restaurant orders, video-shopping sessions, and any custom event a brand wants to pipe in.

Part 2

Where Sagepilot fits

Every one of those tools knows only its own corner. The shop does not know where the truck is. The truck does not know how many points the customer has. So brands hire people to be the glue, opening ten dashboards to answer one message. Sagepilot is the glue, except it is an AI, and it never sleeps.

We are the brain and the mouth. The other tools are the hands and the filing cabinets. We do not replace Shopify, Delhivery, or Razorpay. We sit on top, read everything they know, and turn it into a real conversation and a real action.

Without Sagepilot

A customer messages at 11pm and waits until morning. A human opens ten tabs, hunts for the order, checks the tracking, looks up the refund, takes five minutes, and replies once.

With Sagepilot

The same message at 11pm. The AI already knows the order, the tracking, the payment, the points, and yesterday's angry note. It replies in seconds, fixes the problem itself, and nudges the next sale. While the founder sleeps.

Normally a brand needs several teams. Sagepilot does all five jobs, as one AI, under one roof.

Sales

Turns browsers into buyers.

Support

Answers "where is my order" and solves it.

Retention

Wins people back and keeps them loyal.

Marketing

The right message at the right moment.

The edgeSocial & ORM

Guards the brand's public face on Instagram and Facebook.

Part 3

One brain, eight mouths

Customers do not all show up in the same place, and the brand cannot pick where they talk. So the AI is ready at every door, and it is the same brain at every door, so it remembers you no matter which one you knock on.

The big oneWhatsApp

Where most shopping conversations in India actually happen. Updates, support, and buying right inside the chat.

DiscoveryInstagram

A reel sparks a DM asking "price?" The AI answers and turns the curious DM into a sale.

MetaFacebook Messenger

Same idea, often an older crowd, and where Facebook ad clicks land.

The workhorseEmail

Confirmations, receipts, newsletters, win-backs. Calm and on the record.

Works everywhereSMS

No internet, no app needed. OTPs, delivery updates, COD confirmations, even on a basic phone.

SMS, grown upRCS

A normal text that can show pictures, buttons, and a verified brand name with a logo.

Real callsVoice

The AI can take a call and make one. Confirm a COD order, chase a failed delivery, call back a cart.

On-siteWeb chat

The bubble on the brand's own site, catching the customer at the moment of deciding.

These are not eight separate bots. Ask about a return on WhatsApp today and email about the same order tomorrow, and the AI already knows. Same customer, same memory, every door.

Part 4

Why Sagepilot wins

When a competitor quotes you a number, ask two things: is that your best brand or your average, and across how many brands. That is where the room goes quiet.

The number that ends the argument

0  AI deflection, and that is not one cherry-picked brand on a good day. That is the average across all 500-plus brands we run. Their ceiling is our floor.

The five hammers, use against everyone
1
Deepest context on earth

Most tools read the last few messages and guess. Sagepilot remembers the entire life of the customer, forever. Superhuman context, not a goldfish.

2
It does the job on Shopify

It builds the cart, places the order, confirms it, and changes the delivery address inside Shopify. No other company on the face of the earth does this.

3
Genuinely all five in one

Sales, support, retention, marketing, and social with ORM, as one AI. Almost every competitor is one or two of those wearing a costume.

4
India-native and global

Fluent in COD, RTO, and the Indian stack, smooth worldwide.

5
An AI that does not lie

It knows what it does not know, so it never invents an answer to fill silence. Machine scale, human feel.

Competitor by competitor, tap each one
Marketing & broadcast engine

BiteSpeed

What they actually are. An AI-native marketing engine for WhatsApp, born from cart recovery and campaign blasts. Strong at marketing flows, with support and voice added on top.
  • Their heart is marketing. Ask them to handle deep end-to-end support and act on Shopify like a human, and the seams show.
  • They orchestrate messages. We take actions. Changing an address inside Shopify is a different sport from a well-timed broadcast.
  • Their automation is about open rates. Ours is a 92.3 percent deflection floor across 500-plus brands.
GPT support, enterprise

LimeChat

What they actually are. A GPT-powered support and WhatsApp commerce platform for enterprise. Genuinely strong on support automation. They quote up to 90 percent for their top brands.
  • Read that carefully: 90 percent for their best brands. Our 92.3 percent is the average across 500-plus. Their ceiling is our floor.
  • Support-first with marketing attached. We are all five jobs, equally, under one roof.
  • Their bot answers. Ours acts on Shopify. Resolving a ticket is not building the cart and placing the order.
On-site sales agent

Verifast

What they actually are. An on-site AI sales agent that lives on the product page, multilingual, great at recommendations and recovering carts.
  • They shine on the website. We are everywhere, all eight channels, one brain that remembers across every door.
  • They recommend and nudge toward checkout. We complete it inside Shopify, then handle the delivery, the return, and the next sale.
  • Memory is the gap. A great on-page nudge still forgets you tomorrow. Sagepilot does not.
WhatsApp & Instagram automation

Spur

What they actually are. A clean, affordable WhatsApp and Instagram automation tool, famous for comment-to-DM and cart recovery, built for smaller and mid brands.
  • Spur sends the right message. Sagepilot does the actual work.
  • Their AI can look things up, like order status. Ours can change the address and place the order. Lookups are not actions.
  • Context and memory are shallow there by design. Ours is the deepest on earth.
Agentic CRM, enterprise

BIK

What they actually are. A conversational marketing platform positioning as an agentic AI CRM, with 100-plus pre-built agents. Same house as Manifest AI.
  • They sell a library of 100-plus agents to assemble. We are one employee that already knows the whole customer and acts.
  • “Agentic” on a slide is not the same as actually changing a Shopify address in a live chat. Ask for that demo.
  • Their numbers are campaign ROI. Ours is resolution and action, at 92.3 percent across 500-plus brands.
D2C operating system

Pragma

What they actually are. A D2C operating system strong in post-purchase, returns, tracking, COD and RTO, with a CX and CRM layer and AI that surfaces context for human agents. The closest fight on breadth.
  • Their AI mostly assists the human agent by surfacing context. Ours is the agent, resolving and acting on its own at a 92.3 percent average.
  • Ops and post-purchase first, with conversation layered on. We are conversation and action first, plugged into the full stack including ops.
  • Their strength is a great cockpit for your team. Ours is not needing the team for nine out of ten conversations.
The one-line close

They answer messages, run campaigns, or assist your agents. Sagepilot remembers the customer forever, does the actual work inside Shopify, plays all five roles, guards the brand's public face, speaks India and the world, refuses to lie, and deflects 92.3 percent across 500-plus brands. That is not a better chatbot. That is an employee.

Part 5

The plans

Every plan comes with a pool of AI credits. Think of credits as fuel. Every conversation handled end to end, across any channel, burns a little. More fuel, more conversations.

Pro

$249.99 /mo
20,000 credits
Roughly 3,000 full conversations. Where most brands start.

Scale

$399.99 /mo
32,000 credits
Roughly 4,300 to 4,500 conversations.

Scale Plus

$499.99 /mo
40,000 credits
Roughly 6,000 to 6,200 conversations.

Enterprise

$1,000+ /mo
Custom credits
Custom commercial terms, built around the brand's volume.
Hard rule, memorise this

AI voice calling is never included in any plan. Not Pro, not Scale, not Scale Plus, not Enterprise. Voice is an add-on that sits on top of whichever plan the brand picks, priced separately. Choose a plan for the chat and text conversations, then add voice on top if the brand wants the AI making and taking calls.

The fifth pillar, in full

Social engagement and ORM

ORM means online reputation management, which is just protecting how the brand looks in public. Sitting inside Instagram and Facebook, the AI:

  • Replies to every comment, on posts, reels, stories, and the brand's paid ads, in real context, never a template.
  • Ignores the noise, like an emoji dropped with nothing to answer.
  • Hides or deletes a negative comment instantly, then quietly opens a DM to solve it off the public timeline.
  • Turns any comment or DM into a real buying conversation.
The quiet superpower
CTWA, Click To WhatsApp Ads. When a customer comes in from an ad and starts talking, the AI knows exactly which ad brought them in. The brand finally sees which specific ad drove a real conversation, not just clicks.
